duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Missing authorization check in MarginTradingHook.updateOrder allows any user with a valid position to modify any other user's active order parameters, enabling collateral theft via fillOrder
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All seven threats describe the identical missing `order.initPosId == initPosId` check in updateOrder (present in cancelOrder but absent in updateOrder), with the same attack chain of modify-then-fill. TECH-H001 has the most precise line references and comparison with cancelOrder.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Swapped return value destructuring in _calculateFillOrderInfo causes repayShares and repayAmt to be transposed, breaking all order fills once interest accrues
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All eight threats describe the identical code defect: _calculateRepaySize returns (repayAmt, repayShares) but _calculateFillOrderInfo destructures as (repayShares, repayAmt). TECH-H002 provides the most precise line-level evidence and clearest explanation of the positional return value semantics.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: fillOrder auto-cancel path modifies a memory copy of the order status instead of storage, leaving the order permanently Active despite CancelOrder event emission
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All eight threats describe the identical memory-vs-storage bug: `Order memory order = __orders[_orderId]` followed by `order.status = OrderStatus.Cancelled` modifies memory only. TECH-H003 has the most precise line references and clearest storage/memory distinction. Note: first TECH2-H003 instance (Memory-Only Update) is the one in this group.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Strict equality check `balanceOf(this) == swapInfo.amtOut` in coreCallback CloseExactOut path enables 1-wei dust token griefing DoS on reducePos
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All nine threats describe the identical strict equality check `== swapInfo.amtOut` instead of `>=` that can be broken by 1 wei token donation. ECON-H004 provides the best attack economics analysis including the liquidation profit motive. Note: first TECH2-H004 instance (Strict Equality) is the one in this group.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: LiqIncentiveCalculator returns 0 multiplier when health factor is exactly 0, making underwater positions economically impossible to liquidate and creating permanent bad debt
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All eight threats target the same `if (_healthFactor_e18 == 0) return 0;` early return in getLiqIncentiveMultiplier_e18. ECON2-H010 specifically mentions liquidateWLp but the root cause is identical. ECON-H006 provides the most comprehensive economic analysis and specific code reference.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: LP token price manipulation via flash-loan-funded swap fee accumulation when WLpMoeMasterChef.calculatePrice_e36 uses spot reserves (kLast == 0 branch)
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All five threats describe the same vulnerability in calculatePrice_e36 when kLast==0: live reserves are manipulable via swap fee accumulation. ECON-H007 provides the best attack economics and profit analysis. Note: first TECH2-H008 instance (LP Price) is the one in this group.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: MoneyMarketHook.execute returnNative path sweeps entire WNATIVE/ETH balance to msg.sender, allowing MEV extraction of residual funds from prior transactions
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All seven threats describe the same unprotected balance sweep in MoneyMarketHook.execute. ECON2-H003 additionally mentions MarginTradingHook refundNative but the primary vulnerability is the same cross-transaction fund sweep. ECON-H005 provides the best attack economics. Note: first TECH2-H005 instance (Native Token Sweep) is the one in this group.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Multiple margin orders can over-commit position collateral without aggregate validation across active orders, leading to unexpected proportional scaling on fill
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All five threats describe the identical missing aggregate collateral check in _createOrder: individual `_collAmt <= collAmt` checks pass but sum exceeds total. ECON-H008 provides the most detailed impact analysis. Note: first TECH2-H006 instance (Multiple Orders) is the one in this group.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: InitOracle.getPrice_e36 reverts when both oracle sources return valid prices but maxPriceDeviations_e18 defaults to 0 (not configured), creating a DoS
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All five threats describe the identical configuration trap: maxPriceDeviations_e18 mapping defaults to 0, making the deviation check always fail for positive prices. ECON-H009 provides the most comprehensive analysis including the configuration dependency. Note: first TECH2-H007 instance (Oracle Deviation) is the one in this group.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: MarginTradingHook.reducePos missing `_require(initPosId != 0)` check unlike all other position-modifying functions, allowing operations on default position 0
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All five threats describe the identical missing existence check in reducePos compared to increasePos/addCollateral/removeCollateral/repayDebt. TECH-H010 provides the most thorough comparison across all sibling functions. Note: second TECH2-H003 instance (reducePos initPosId=0) is the one in this group.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: WLpMoeMasterChef first depositor loses initial rewards because updateRewards modifier captures lpSupply=0 before deposit, skipping reward crediting
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both threats describe the identical reward loss when lpSupply==0 on first wrap for a pid. TECH-H012 provides more specific line references for the updateRewards modifier.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: InitCore.callback is publicly callable with no access control, enabling arbitrary external calls from CORE context to any ICallbackReceiver
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All three threats describe the same permissionless callback function with only `_to != address(this)` guard. TECH-H014 provides the most comprehensive analysis of the confused-deputy risk. Note: second TECH2-H004 instance (InitCore.callback) is the one in this group.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: InitCore.transferToken is an unprotected public token-pulling primitive that expands attack surface for approval abuse in composable flows
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All three threats describe the same public `safeTransferFrom(msg.sender, _to, _amt)` function with no access control. TECH-H015 provides the most detailed analysis of multicall composition risks. Note: second TECH2-H005 instance (transferToken) is the one in this group.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: WLpMoeMasterChef.getPendingRewards divides by lpSupply without zero-guard, causing revert when lpSupply==0
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both threats describe the identical division-by-zero in getPendingRewards when lpSupply==0. TECH-H016 provides more detailed analysis of potential propagation into critical paths.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: MarginTradingHook.coreCallback uses balanceOf(this) to determine swap input amount, sweeping all residual tokens into the current user's swap
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All three threats describe the identical use of `IERC20(swapInfo.tokenIn).balanceOf(address(this))` instead of a tracked amount, enabling theft of stuck ERC20 tokens. ECON2-H004 provides the best economic analysis of the extraction opportunity.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: MarginTradingHook.initialize is publicly callable without role restrictions, allowing a front-runner to set a malicious swapHelper
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both threats describe the identical unprotected initializer for swapHelper. OPS-H013 provides a more comprehensive attack chain analysis showing how coreCallback transfers all tokens to swapHelper.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: PosManager.addCollateral uses balance-delta accounting (balanceOf - __collBalances) that can be manipulated by direct token transfers/donations to misattribute collateral
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All four threats describe the identical balance-delta pattern in addCollateral where `amtIn = newBalance - __collBalances[_pool]` includes externally donated tokens. ECON-H020 provides the best attack economics and front-running scenario. Note: second TECH2-H006 instance (PosManager balance-delta) is the one in this group.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Flash loans are feeless (InitCore.flash only checks balance recovery) and atomic borrow/repay via multicall accrues zero interest, providing free attack capital amplification
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both threats describe the same issue of costless flash borrowing. ECON-H010 focuses on the flash function's lack of fees; TECH2-H011 focuses on atomic borrow/repay via multicall. Both are manifestations of the same zero-cost capital amplification. ECON-H010 provides better economic context.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: MoneyMarketHook processes msg.value in a loop without tracking remaining ETH, causing revert when multiple WNATIVE operations are included in a single execute call
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both threats describe the identical pattern of msg.value being consumed on first iteration of the loop. ECON2-H008 covers _handleDeposit and ECON2-H013 covers _handleRepay, but the root cause (msg.value reuse in loop) and fix are the same. ECON2-H008 is selected as the representative.
